name: Breaker replacement (standard)
description: Replace a failed or damaged standard breaker with a new matching breaker.
labor_notes: Swap, torque, and load test; assumes the panel accepts current-production breakers (flag obsolete brands).

name: Breaker replacement (GFCI)
description: Replace a failed GFCI breaker with a new self-test GFCI breaker.
labor_notes: GFCI breakers add the neutral pigtail and a downstream trip test over a standard swap.

name: Breaker replacement (AFCI)
description: Replace a failed AFCI breaker with a new combination AFCI breaker.
labor_notes: Same handling as the GFCI swap, plus a nuisance-trip check with the circuit's typical loads.

name: Receptacle replacement
description: Replace a damaged, worn, or discolored receptacle with a new tamper-resistant device.
labor_notes: Includes checking the box's conductors for heat damage while it's open — the part that matters more than the swap.

name: Switch replacement
description: Replace a failed or worn switch with a new device.
labor_notes: Straightforward swap; multi-way or dimmer replacements use their own assemblies.

name: Junction box violation repair
description: Correct an open-splice or buried-junction violation: install a proper accessible box, re-make splices, and cover.
labor_notes: Re-making old splices safely and mounting an accessible box takes longer than it looks, especially in attics and crawls.

name: Panel cover (dead-front) replacement
description: Replace a missing or damaged panel dead-front cover with a correct-fit replacement.
labor_notes: Sourcing the right cover is the hard part (handled at quote time); on-site fit and screw-up is quick.

name: Double-tap breaker correction
description: Correct a double-tapped breaker by adding a properly rated tandem breaker or pigtail so each conductor lands correctly.
labor_notes: One of the most common inspection findings; corrected with a tandem (where the panel allows) or a listed pigtail splice.

name: Hardwired smoke/CO detector replacement (first)
description: Replace a hardwired smoke/CO detector with a new combination unit with battery backup (first unit).
labor_notes: First unit includes verifying the interconnect signal works across the system after replacement.

name: Hardwired smoke/CO detector replacement (each additional)
description: Each additional hardwired smoke/CO detector replaced during the same visit.
labor_notes: Marginal time per unit; whole-house replacements are common since detectors age out together.

name: Doorbell transformer replacement
description: Replace a failed or undersized doorbell transformer (common for video doorbell upgrades).
labor_notes: Locating the existing transformer is often the longest step — they hide in panels, attics, and closets.
